tasks.title: Bridge-side: subscribe to meshsat/relay/{bridge_id}/+ + forward to localhost HTTP + directory_version in health (S9-06, S9-07)
tasks.prompt: FIRST: read spec/009-multi-bridge-nat/{requirements.md, design.md, acceptance/multi-bridge-nat.feature}. SECOND: (REQ-813) extend internal/hubreporter/ to subscribe to meshsat/relay/{bridge_id}/+ on the Hub MQTT connection. On inbound frames, forward the inner HTTP request to localhost:6050 as if from LAN — frames are mTLS-encrypted; the bridge's local NATS terminates. (REQ-814) bridge health payload from existing internal/sysinfo/ adds directory_version + last_sync. (REQ-820) ensure pair-protocol response includes rns_dest + hub_relay_url so paired_bridges rows populate on initial pairing.

tasks.title: Browser-as-remote-control /pair/scan view + diagnostic ring buffer endpoint (S9-08 partial)
tasks.prompt: FIRST: read spec/009-multi-bridge-nat/{requirements.md, design.md}. SECOND: add web/src/views/PairScanView.vue exposing the webcam-driven QR scan per UX-MULTI-ACCESS-KIOSK-PAIRING.md §6 — JWT in sessionStorage; client private key in IndexedDB as non-extractable CryptoKey. (REQ-815/REQ-816) implement bridge-side endpoint GET /api/transport/diagnostics returning the 100-event ring buffer (for operator-visible Settings → Diagnostics on Android side; bridge holds the canonical log per session).

tasks.title: End-to-end integration test: Android cellular-only → Hub-relay → CG-NAT bridge (S9-09)
tasks.prompt: FIRST: read spec/009-multi-bridge-nat/{requirements.md, design.md, acceptance/multi-bridge-nat.feature}. SECOND: add test/integration/multi_bridge_nat_test.go simulating: an Android client without LAN+RNS reachability to a CG-NAT-bridge; bridge subscribes to its relay topic via Hub; Android dispatches Compose → Hub relay → bridge → outbound MQTT. Use the meshsat-hub Hub-relay mock (this test is bridge-side; complete e2e crosses repos but this side verifies the bridge's contract). (REQ-817) confirms full round-trip.
